# opnsense_quagga_bfd_neighbor (Resource)

Configure BFD neighbors.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "opnsense_quagga_bfd_neighbor" "example" {
  address     = "192.0.2.1"
  description = "BFD neighbor to router"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `address` (String) The IPv4 or IPv6 address of the BFD neighbor.

### Optional

- `description` (String) An optional description for this BFD neighbor. Defaults to `""`.
- `detect_multiplier` (Number) The detection multiplier. Defaults to `3`.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable this BFD neighbor. Defaults to `true`.
- `interface_name` (String) The interface used for the BFD session. Defaults to `""`.
- `local_address` (String) The local address used for the BFD session. Defaults to `""`.
- `multi_hop` (Boolean) Enable multi-hop BFD. Defaults to `false`.
- `receive_interval` (Number) The minimum receive interval in milliseconds. Defaults to `300`.
- `transmit_interval` (Number) The minimum transmit interval in milliseconds. Defaults to `300`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the BFD neighbor.

## Import

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import opnsense_quagga_bfd_neighbor using the `id`. For example:

```terraform
import {
  to = opnsense_quagga_bfd_neighbor.example
  id = "<opnsense-resource-id>"
}
```

Using `terraform import`, import opnsense_quagga_bfd_neighbor using the `id`. For example:

```console
% terraform import opnsense_quagga_bfd_neighbor.example <opnsense-resource-id>
```
